hero.description: Mechatronics Engineering graduate focused on the full robotics stack, including mechanical systems, electronics, embedded control, automation, and software integration.

about.title: About
about.content: I am a Graduate Mechatronics Engineer from Nelson Mandela University, with a strong foundation in mechanical, electrical, and software systems. My practical experience comes from academic and personal projects, where I designed, built, and tested electromechanical systems. I am passionate about solving real-world engineering problems and seeking opportunities to grow, learn, and contribute in multidisciplinary environments.

items.description:
• Supported bursary recipients to stay focused on their academic and career goals, improving program outcomes.
• Shared practical strategies for studying and time management that peers could apply immediately.
• Encouraged resilience by helping others develop coping mechanisms.
• Nurtured community bonds that improved morale and collective accountability.
• Learned how active listening and empathy improve collaboration and problem solving.

items.company: Nelson Mandela University, Gqeberha, South Africa
items.description:
• Guided first year students in adjusting to the demands of university, reducing drop-out risk through academic and social support.
• Facilitated sessions on study skills and campus resources that gave students practical tools to succeed.
• Organised tours that improved awareness of university services and fostered belonging.
• Offered continuous one-to-one support, improving student confidence and integration.
• Strengthened leadership and mentoring skills while making a visible impact on student well-being.

items.company: Nelson Mandela University, Gqeberha, South Africa
items.description:
• Translated difficult concepts into accessible explanations, helping students raise their marks.
• Produced learning materials that improved clarity and supported self-study.
• Provided targeted assistance that helped weaker students gain confidence and independence.
• Engaged groups in structured tutorials that encouraged participation.
• Improved my technical communication by making abstract topics understandable to varied learners.

• Assisted in machining operations that directly contributed to product components.
• Participated in fabrication and assembly processes that demonstrated the link between design and production.
• Applied workshop practices that improved my accuracy and safety awareness.
• Learned the practical implications of design decisions by working alongside technicians.
• Strengthened teamwork through collaboration with engineers across departments.

items.company: Volkswagen South Africa (VWSA), Uitenhage, South Africa
items.description:
• Completed structured training in mechanics, electronics, and PLCs that built a strong foundation in automation.
• Constructed and tested circuits that demonstrate how theoretical concepts translate into industrial practice.
• Practiced diagnostic methods and workshop routines that increased technical competence.
• Gained first exposure to PLC programming, building confidence in automation systems.
• Observed professional standards in a large industrial setting, preparing for future engineering roles.

items.title: Collaborative Oil Spill Containment System using Unmanned Surface Vehicles (USVs)
items.tech: Raspberry Pi 5, Pixhawk Flight Controller, T200 thrusters, Python, OpenCV (Image Processing)
items.description: Developed autonomous USVs to detect and corral oil spills in real time, addressing the limitations of traditional booms and skimmers. A leader USV with vision-based perception guides two slave USVs using a 'sheepherding' strategy. Image data is processed on a Raspberry Pi, and a Pixhawk controller actuates the thrusters. The system confines oil for treatment while integrating mechanical and electrical design, demonstrating scalable autonomous containment and environmental monitoring.

items.title: 2D Robotic Arm Artist
items.tech: Forward Kinematics, Inverse Kinematics, Robot Manipulation, JavaScript, HTML, CSS
items.description: Developed a prototype robotic arm that accepts an image and attempts to reproduce it stroke by stroke. Motion control uses forward and inverse kinematics, applying principles from Advanced Manufacturing Systems modules. The project is ongoing, providing hands-on experience with robotics, kinematics, and automated manufacturing concepts.

items.title: Temperature Control System
items.tech: C, Arduino, Temperature Sensors, Motors (Fans), LCD Display
items.description: Designed and built a temperature-controlled fan system using an Arduino Uno and TMP35 sensor to maintain desired temperature thresholds. The system uses PWM-based fan speed control. I independently handled the full project lifecycle, including circuit design, sensor interfacing, coding, and testing. I addressed practical challenges such as voltage-induced sensor reading disruptions and provided real-time temperature feedback through an LCD display.

items.title: Water Tank Control System – PLC & HMI Development (Siemens TIA Portal)
items.description: Developed a PLC control system for an automated water-level tank as part of a group project using Siemens TIA Portal. Ladder logic was implemented to manage fill and drain operations based on low, mid, and high-level sensor inputs, while an HMI interface was designed to provide real-time level indication, manual and automatic pump control, alarms, and safety interlocks. The system was simulated within TIA Portal to validate control logic and fault responses, with timing refinements ensuring smooth transitions between idle, filling, and draining states to enhance overall stability.

items.title: First PCB
items.description: Developed a printed circuit board using KiCad, focusing on layout, routing, and adherence to PCB design rules. While the underlying circuits and schematics were already well understood, the project focused on mastering the complexities of PCB design, including trace routing, component placement, spacing, and overall board organization. The board was not physically fabricated, but the exercise provided valuable experience in translating functional circuits into manufacturable PCB layouts.

items.title: Gantry System Improvement (Isuzu Project)
items.description: Collaborated in a two-person team to optimize an industrial gantry system, reducing downtime and production delays. Contributed to process mapping, CAD design, system modeling, and project planning. Proposed a redundancy solution with a standby unit to ensure continuous operation during maintenance and introduced buffer mechanisms to stabilize production flow within real-world space and budget constraints.

items.title: Game Engine for 2D Games
items.description: Developing a lightweight 2D game engine using vanilla JavaScript, HTML Canvas, and CSS. The engine handles core game loops, collision detection, sprite rendering, and input management. This project deepens my understanding of game development architecture and real-time performance optimization.

items.title: Software Prototypes
items.description: A collection of web-based prototypes including chess games, snake game, tic-tac-toe, and other interactive applications. These projects were developed as part of my software engineering learning process, focusing on experimentation, debugging, and improving code structure over time.

items.title: Structure Simulator
items.tech: Mechanical Engineering Principles, Simulation Algorithms, HTML, CSS, JavaScript
items.description: Designed and developed a web-based structure simulator using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The simulator allows users to create and analyze simple structures under various loads, providing real-time feedback on stress distribution and deformation. This project serves as a practical application of structural engineering principles and offers an interactive platform for learning and experimentation.

items.title: Circuit Simulator
items.tech: Electrical Engineering Principles, Simulation Algorithms, HTML, CSS, JavaScript
items.description: Designed and developed a web-based circuit simulator using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. The simulator allows users to create and analyze simple circuits under various conditions, providing real-time feedback on current flow and voltage distribution. This project serves as a practical application of electrical engineering principles and offers an interactive platform for learning and experimentation.
